findings.title: Vague instruction to submit aggregated metrics externally
findings.description: The 'Advanced: Cross-Repository Benchmarking' section instructs: 'Submit the aggregated metrics to your organization's CI dashboard.' This is ambiguous but could be interpreted by the agent as sending collected data (workflow metadata, billing info) to an unknown external service. While no concrete endpoint or method is provided, the instruction itself carries a latent exfiltration risk if the agent blindly executes. No script code is present to confirm actual network calls, but the design encourages data aggregation and potential external submission without user confirmation.
findings.evidence: Text: 'Submit the aggregated metrics to your organization's CI dashboard for trend tracking and team-level reporting.'
findings.remediation: Clarify that 'submission' is a manual step for the user, or explicitly state that no data leaves the local environment. Alternatively, add a confirmation prompt before any data is transmitted.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools declaration
findings.description: The YAML manifest does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field. This is optional per the skill specification, so it is not a security violation. However, the absence means the agent has no explicit restriction on which tools can be used for this skill (e.g., Bash, Python, Write). The skill relies heavily on Bash commands (via `gh`), which are implicitly permitted. Informational only.
findings.remediation: Consider declaring an explicit tool whitelist (e.g., `allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Grep]`) to align with the principle of least privilege.
